instruction: Implement a soft deprecation warning for readr functions when literal data is passed without wrapping it in `I()`. The vroom package (a dependency) now requires `I()` for literal data, and readr needs to handle this gracefully by emitting its own soft deprecation warning and automatically wrapping the data in `I()`. Create a helper function `standardise_literal_data()` that detects literal data (single character strings containing newlines that aren't already wrapped in `I()`), emits a soft deprecation warning using `lifecycle::deprecate_soft()`, and wraps the data in `I()`. Apply this helper to `read_delim()`, `read_csv()`, `read_csv2()`, `read_tsv()`, and `read_fwf()`.
context:
The helper function should:
- Check if `file` is a single character string containing a newline (`\n`)
- Check that it doesn't already inherit from class 'AsIs' (which `I()` adds)
- Use `lifecycle::deprecate_soft()` with appropriate version, function name, and helpful examples
- Return `I(file)` for literal data, otherwise return `file` unchanged

Function signature pattern:
r
standardise_literal_data <- function(file, fn, env, user_env) {
  # detection logic here
  lifecycle::deprecate_soft(...)
  I(file)
}


Call pattern in read functions:
r
file <- standardise_literal_data(
  file,
  "read_csv",
  env = current_env(),
  user_env = caller_env()
)

fail_to_pass: read_csv("x,y\n1,2") should emit a lifecycle deprecation warning about using I() for literal data
read_csv2("x;y\n1;2") should emit a lifecycle deprecation warning about using I() for literal data
read_tsv("x\ty\n1\t2") should emit a lifecycle deprecation warning about using I() for literal data
read_delim("x|y\n1|2", delim = "|") should emit a lifecycle deprecation warning about using I() for literal data
read_fwf("abcd\nefgh", fwf_widths(c(2, 2))) should emit a lifecycle deprecation warning about using I() for literal data
pass_to_pass: read_csv(I("x,y\n1,2")) should NOT emit any deprecation warning
read_csv2(I("x;y\n1;2")) should NOT emit a deprecation warning
read_tsv(I("x\ty\n1\t2")) should NOT emit any deprecation warning
read_delim(I("x|y\n1|2"), delim = "|") should NOT emit any deprecation warning
read_fwf(I("abcd\nefgh"), fwf_widths(c(2, 2))) should NOT emit any deprecation warning
read_csv(path_to_actual_file) should NOT emit a deprecation warning (file paths should be distinguished from literal data)

key_changes: Detect literal data: `is.character(file) && length(file) == 1 && grepl("\n", file) && !inherits(file, "AsIs")`
Use `lifecycle::deprecate_soft()` with version '2.2.0', function name, and helpful bad/good examples
Apply helper to read_delim, read_csv, read_csv2, read_tsv (in R/read_delim.R) and read_fwf (in R/read_fwf.R)
Update documentation to clarify that I() should be used for literal data
potential_pitfalls: Not checking for 'AsIs' class will cause double-wrapping or infinite warnings
Not distinguishing file paths from literal data - file paths should NOT trigger the warning
Using wrong environment parameters in deprecate_soft() will show incorrect source location
Forgetting to apply the helper to all relevant read functions
Not handling the case where file is a vector with multiple elements
solution.reference_diff:
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index aded0a9e..eb389b01 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -1,5 +1,7 @@
 # readr (development version)
 
+* `read_csv()`, `read_csv2()`, `read_tsv()`, `read_delim()`, and `read_fwf()` now emit a deprecation warning when literal data is passed without wrapping it in `I()`. For example, usage like `read_csv(I("x,y\n1,2"))` is preferred over `read_csv("x,y\n1,2")` (#1611).
+
 * `read_table2()` has been removed from readr. This function was deprecated in
   readr 2.0.0 (2021-07-20) in favor of `read_table()`. Use `read_table()`
   instead.

--- a/R/read_delim.R
+++ b/R/read_delim.R
@@ -267,6 +267,13 @@ read_delim <- function(
     )
   }
 
+  file <- standardise_literal_data(
+    file,
+    "read_delim",
+    env = current_env(),
+    user_env = caller_env()
+  )
+
   vroom::vroom(
     file,
     delim = delim,
@@ -359,6 +366,14 @@ read_csv <- function(
       )
     )
   }
+
+  file <- standardise_literal_data(
+    file,
+    "read_csv",
+    env = current_env(),
+    user_env = caller_env()
+  )
+
   vroom::vroom(
     file,
     delim = ",",
@@ -457,6 +472,14 @@ read_csv2 <- function(
       )
     )
   }
+
+  file <- standardise_literal_data(
+    file,
+    "read_csv2",
+    env = current_env(),
+    user_env = caller_env()
+  )
+
   vroom::vroom(
     file,
     delim = ";",
@@ -548,6 +571,13 @@ read_tsv <- function(
     )
   }
 
+  file <- standardise_literal_data(
+    file,
+    "read_tsv",
+    env = current_env(),
+    user_env = caller_env()
+  )
+
   vroom::vroom(
     file,
     delim = "\t",
@@ -575,6 +605,40 @@ read_tsv <- function(
 }
 
 # Helper functions for reading from delimited files ----------------------------
+
+# This became necessary when vroom 1.7.0 moved up to deprecate_warn() for
+# literal data that was not wrapped in I().
+#
+# We don't want readr users:
+# * To go abruptly from no message to deprecate_warn()
+# * To get a message from vroom that tells them to open an issue on readr
+standardise_literal_data <- function(file, fn, env, user_env) {
+  if (
+    is.character(file) &&
+      length(file) == 1 &&
+      grepl("\n", file) &&
+      !inherits(file, "AsIs")
+  ) {
+    lifecycle::deprecate_soft(
+      "2.2.0",
+      paste0(fn, "(file = 'should use `I()` for literal data')"),
+      details = c(
+        " " = "",
+        " " = "# Bad (for example):",
+        " " = 'read_csv("x,y\\n1,2")',
+        " " = "",
+        " " = "# Good:",
+        " " = 'read_csv(I("x,y\\n1,2"))'
+      ),
+      env = env,
+      user_env = user_env
+    )
+    I(file)
+  } else {
+    file
+  }
+}
+
 read_tokens <- function(
   data,
   tokenizer,
diff --git a/R/read_fwf.R b/R/read_fwf.R
index a22bb907..5f9da631 100644
--- a/R/read_fwf.R
+++ b/R/read_fwf.R
@@ -143,6 +143,13 @@ read_fwf <- function(
     return(warn_problems(out))
   }
 
+  file <- standardise_literal_data(
+    file,
+    "read_fwf",
+    env = current_env(),
+    user_env = caller_env()
+  )
+
   vroom::vroom_fwf(
     file,
     col_positions = col_positions,

--- a/R/source.R
+++ b/R/source.R
@@ -10,9 +10,7 @@
 #'    decompressed.
 #'
 #'    Literal data is most useful for examples and tests. To be recognised as
-#'    literal data, the input must be either wrapped with `I()`, be a string
-#'    containing at least one new line, or be a vector containing at least one
-#'    string with a new line.
+#'    literal data, wrap the input with `I()`.
 #'
 #'    Using a value of [clipboard()] will read from the system clipboard.
 #'

-containing at least one new line, or be a vector containing at least one
-string with a new line.

 Using a value of \code{\link[=clipboard]{clipboard()}} will read from the system clipboard.}
